answer:
The energy stored in an inductor is given by \( W = \int i \, d\phi \). Since \( \phi = \frac{1}{3} i^3 \), we have \( d\phi = i^2 \, di \), so \( W = \int i \cdot i^2 \, di = \int i^3 \, di \). With \( i(t) = 2\sin t \), the energy from \( t=0 \) to \( t=1 \) is \( \int_{0}^{1} (2\sin t)^3 \cdot (2\cos t \, dt) = 16 \int_{0}^{1} \sin^3 t \cos t \, dt = 4 \left[ \sin^4 t \right]_{0}^{1} = 4 \sin^4(1) \approx 4(0.8415^4) \approx 2.00 \, \text{J} \).  

\
